outcome_text: Resources required to manage AI risks are taken into account—along with viable non-AI alternative systems, approaches, or methods—to reduce the magnitude or likelihood of potential impacts.
scoring_text: Ability to conduct AI resource and alternative analysis, including assessing resources needed for AI risk management, evaluating non-AI alternative systems and approaches, and making comparative decisions that reduce potential impact magnitude and likelihood.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to conduct AI resource and alternative analysis, including assessing resources needed for AI risk management, evaluating non-AI alternative systems and approaches, and making comparative decisions that reduce potential impact magnitude and likelihood.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: NIST AI RMF MG-2.1 (MANAGE) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.719, cosine: 0.742). Secondary: AG-T-009 (AG, cosine: 0.688), RM-A-001 (RM, cosine: 0.681).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.2/10 (raw 0.719). Shared vocabulary: [ai, impact, risk]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, alternative, analysis, approaches, assessing, comparative] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.887 (P=0.878, R=0.895). Balanced P/R — the 33-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.742 ≈ STS 0.719 (gap 0.023). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, impact, risk] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, impact, risk]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, alternative, analysis, approaches, assessing, comparative]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation].
